hero.tagline: High-quality pediatric dental care that kids actually look forward to — serving families throughout Lake County.
hero.trustBanner: Serving Lake County for Over 30 Years · Est. 1994

services.heading: Our Services
services.body: From routine check-ups to specialized treatments, we provide comprehensive, compassionate dental care designed specifically for children.

preventive.title: Preventive Dentistry
preventive.description: Regular check-ups, cleanings, and fluoride treatments to keep your child's smile healthy and stop problems before they start.

restorative.title: Restorative Dentistry
restorative.description: Expert, gentle treatment for cavities and broken teeth to restore healthy smiles using child-friendly techniques.

sedation.title: Sedation Dentistry
sedation.description: Gentle sedation options for nervous kids and more complex procedures, including nitrous oxide and general anesthesia.

specialNeeds.title: Special Needs Dentistry
specialNeeds.description: Compassionate, specialized care for children with diverse needs — including autism, Down syndrome, ADHD, and sensory differences.

emergency.title: Emergency Pediatric Dentistry
emergency.description: When your child has a toothache, knocked-out tooth, or dental injury, we'll get them in as soon as possible.

insurance.heading: In-Network with Most Major PPOs
insurance.body: We accept most major dental insurance plans to make care affordable for your family.
insurance.footerLink: Text/Call us — we likely accept it.

doctors.heading: Meet Our Doctors
doctors.body: Four pediatric specialists who genuinely love what they do. Get to know the team your family will see at every visit.

valueProps.heading: A Practice Built Around Your Child
valueProps.body: We designed every part of our practice, from our schedule to our bedside manner with young patients and their parents in mind.

experts.title: Specialized pediatric experts
experts.body: All four of our board-certified doctors focus exclusively on pediatric dentistry — care designed specifically for little mouths and big feelings.

environment.title: Child-friendly environment
environment.body: Our office is designed to delight and comfort kids at every turn — from colorful spaces to gentle voices that make every dental visit something to look forward to.

comms.title: Parent-centered communication
comms.body: Transparent treatment plans, plain-language explanations, and zero judgment. You'll always know exactly what's happening with your child's smile.

floating.ariaAppointment: Request an appointment at Kids Dentist Grayslake

footer.tagline: Caring for kids' smiles in Grayslake since day one.

common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

aboutPage.title: Thirty Years of Taking Care of Lake County Kids
aboutPage.whoWeAreBody: Kids Dentist opened in Grayslake in 1994. We've watched this community grow up. We've treated kids who now bring their own kids in. Our four doctors have spent their entire careers focused on one thing: pediatric dentistry.
aboutPage.teamBody: Every doctor at Kids Dentist completed a pediatric dental residency after dental school, which means extra training specifically in treating children, including kids with anxiety, sensory sensitivities, and special needs.
aboutPage.specialNeedsHeading: Experienced Care for Children with Special Needs
aboutPage.specialNeedsBody: Some children need a little more time, a quieter environment, or a gentler approach. Our team has been caring for children with special needs since 1994 and we know how to adjust to meet each child where they are. If your child has had a hard time at the dentist before, please reach out before scheduling. We would love the chance to make this a better experience for them.
aboutPage.specialNeedsLink: Learn about our Special Needs program
aboutPage.sedationBody: Many children feel nervous before a dental visit, and that is completely normal. Our team is experienced in working with anxious patients and takes the time to build trust before beginning any treatment. For children who need additional support, we offer nitrous oxide and general anesthesia. We will always discuss the options with you beforehand and recommend only what is appropriate for your child.
aboutPage.insuranceHeading: We Work with Your Insurance So You Don't Have To
aboutPage.insuranceBody: We're in-network with most major PPOs. If you're not sure whether we take your plan, call or text us. Our front desk handles the paperwork and will tell you exactly what's covered before we start any treatment. No surprise bills.
aboutPage.spaceBody: Step inside and you will instantly feel the difference — warm, colorful, and designed from the ground up with little ones in mind.
aboutPage.slide1Caption: TVs in every operatory — kids choose what they watch
aboutPage.slide2Caption: A dedicated space to ease nerves before any appointment
aboutPage.slide3Caption: Hired for warmth as much as their clinical skill
aboutPage.eventsBody: We're proud to support Lake County families beyond the dental chair — through school visits, sports sponsorships, and community events.
aboutPage.ctaBody: Request an appointment online and we'll call you within one business day to confirm.

servicesPage.title: Our Services
servicesPage.subtitle: 100% pediatric dentistry - because children aren't just small adults.
servicesPage.service0Desc: Routine cleanings, fluoride treatments, sealants, and digital X-rays form the foundation of a lifetime of healthy smiles. We recommend visits every six months starting at age one.
servicesPage.service1Tagline: Fix it early - protect it for life.
servicesPage.service1Desc: Tooth-colored fillings, pediatric crowns, pulp therapy, and extractions - all performed with the gentle approach and honest communication children and parents deserve.
servicesPage.service2Tagline: Calm, comfortable care for anxious kids.
servicesPage.service2Desc: We offer nitrous oxide and general anesthesia — because no child should ever avoid necessary dental care due to fear or anxiety.
servicesPage.service3Desc: Specialized experience treating children with autism, Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, and sensory processing differences.
servicesPage.service4Tagline: Fast care when your child needs it most.
servicesPage.service4Desc: Knocked-out tooth, sudden toothache, or dental trauma - call us immediately. Same-day emergency appointments are available for our patients and for new families in need.
servicesPage.insuranceBody: We are in-network with Aetna, Cigna, Delta Dental Premier, Guardian, Lincoln Financial, Principal, and United Healthcare. Our team handles the billing paperwork — you focus on your child.
servicesPage.bottomCtaText: Not sure which service your child needs? We will help you figure it out.

firstVisit.title: Your Child's First Visit
firstVisit.subtitle: We make first visits something kids actually look forward to.
firstVisit.ageRuleHeading: The Age One Dental Visit - Why It Matters
firstVisit.ageRuleBodyPrefix: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry and the team at Kids Dentist recommend scheduling your child's first dental visit by the time their
firstVisit.ageRuleBodyBold: first tooth erupts, or before they reach the age of one
firstVisit.ageRuleBodySuffix: — whichever comes first. This is not about treatment — it is about building a relationship, checking that development is on track, and giving you the guidance to protect your child's smile from day one. The earlier we start, the healthier the long-term outcome.
firstVisit.videoHeading: Take a Tour Before You Arrive
firstVisit.stepsHeading: Four Steps to a Happy First Visit
firstVisit.step0Desc: From the moment you walk in, our front office team will greet you by name, process your forms, and give your child space to explore our kid-scaled spaces.
firstVisit.step1Desc: We count your child's teeth and perform a gentle, non-threatening visual exam to ensure development is on track. Our doctors use soft, kid-friendly words and go slow to show them that visiting the dentist isn't scary.
firstVisit.step2Desc: Around age two, we introduce a professional cleaning (prophylaxis) followed by flossing and polishing, and apply a safe, concentrated topical fluoride varnish at the end of the appointment for an extra boost of cavity protection.
firstVisit.step3Desc: Every patient picks a sticker and a token for a prize out of the toy tower before they leave — the best way to end any dental appointment!
firstVisit.bringHeading: What to Bring to Your First Visit
firstVisit.bring0: Dental insurance card and policy details
firstVisit.bring1: Your child's medical history — medications, allergies, and prior hospitalizations
firstVisit.bring2: A comfort object — a stuffed animal, blanket, or pacifier are always welcome
firstVisit.bring3: Any prior dental records, if this is not your child's very first dental visit
firstVisit.bring4: A parent or legal guardian must be present for all first-time visits
firstVisit.prep0: Talk about visiting a children's dentist using positive words and tones. Your child will pick up on any anxiety or negative language — avoid words like "hurt," "drill," or "shot."
firstVisit.prep1: Read kid-friendly books about visiting the dentist together to help minimize anxiety before the day arrives.
firstVisit.prep2: Show them pictures of our friendly, child-scaled office atmosphere so the space feels familiar when they walk in.
firstVisit.prep3: Clearly explain what will happen during the first visit so there are no surprises on the day.
firstVisit.quote: If your child leaves our office smiling and asking when they come back, we have done our job exactly right.
firstVisit.ctaText: Ready to schedule the visit that starts it all?
firstVisit.prep4: Role-play 'dentist' at home! Swap roles with your child and practice counting teeth together — it makes the real appointment feel familiar before it happens.

insurancePage.title: Insurance & Financing
insurancePage.subtitle: We handle the paperwork. You focus on your child.
insurancePage.dontSeePrefix: Don't see your plan? Call us - we likely accept it.
insurancePage.outOfNetworkBody: Even if we are not in your insurance network, we want to help you maximize your benefits. Many families are surprised by how much their out-of-network plan covers.
insurancePage.outOfNetwork0: We will file your claim directly to your insurance company on your behalf.
insurancePage.outOfNetwork1: You pay only your estimated patient portion at the time of service.
insurancePage.outOfNetwork2: Our team will work to maximize every dollar of your out-of-network benefits.
insurancePage.outOfNetwork3: We provide a detailed treatment summary for any appeals you need to file.
insurancePage.financingBody: We work with third-party financing providers to offer flexible payment plans for treatment that insurance does not fully cover. Ask our team about available options at your next visit.
insurancePage.financingCardBody: Low- and no-interest plans available - ask us for details
insurancePage.uninsuredBody: We never turn a child away based on insurance status. Our team will work with you on payment arrangements and connect you with available state assistance programs if needed. Every child in our community deserves excellent dental care - full stop. Call us and let us figure it out together.
insurancePage.ctaText: Questions about your coverage? We love talking about this stuff.

faqPage.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqPage.subtitle: Everything parents ask — answered clearly and honestly.
faqPage.askPromptBody: Send it directly to our doctors — we respond within one business day.
faqPage.group0q0: When is a good time for my child's first dental visit?
faqPage.group0a0: In most cases, we recommend scheduling your child's first dental appointment around their first birthday, or within six months after their first tooth erupts — whichever comes first. This initial visit is designed to be highly positive and low-stress. Our primary focus is helping your baby feel comfortable with our team, performing a quick, gentle visual exam, and discussing teething tips and at-home oral care to set them up for a lifetime of healthy routines.
faqPage.group0q1: Why should I choose a pediatric dentist?
faqPage.group0a1: A pediatric dentist completes extensive, specialized post-graduate training — a 2-year residency after dental school — focused entirely on the unique physical, emotional, and developmental needs of children at every stage. All of our doctors are Board-Certified Pediatric Dentists (Diplomates of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry), enabling us to provide tailored behavioral guidance, sensory accommodations, and sedation care that you won't find at a general dental practice.
faqPage.group0q2: Can I stay with my child during their appointment?
faqPage.group0a2: Yes! Parents are always welcome to stay with their children throughout their entire appointment. We believe that building trust with both the child and parent requires complete transparency — so there is zero separation and no surprises in our treatment rooms.
faqPage.group1q0: Are dental X-rays safe for children?
faqPage.group1a0: Yes, absolutely. At Kids Dentist, we utilize the safest, state-of-the-art digital X-ray sensors, which reduce radiation exposure by up to 90% compared to traditional film-based dental X-rays. In addition, we always employ strict protection protocols, including the standard use of lead aprons and protective thyroid collars.
faqPage.group1q1: Why fix a cavity in a baby tooth if it's going to fall out anyway?
faqPage.group1a1: Baby teeth play several critical roles in your child's physical development: they serve as natural space savers that guide permanent teeth into proper alignment, and they are essential for normal chewing, nutrition, and healthy speech development. If left untreated, decay in baby teeth can cause severe toothaches, lead to deep infections that affect the underlying permanent buds, or cause premature tooth loss, resulting in teeth drifting and severe crowding that requires intensive orthodontics later.
faqPage.group1q2: When and why is it good to use fluoride?
faqPage.group1a2: Fluoride is a natural mineral that strengthens tooth enamel upon contact, making it highly resistant to acid attacks and tooth decay. Because excessive ingestion can cause cosmetic staining (fluorosis) in developing permanent teeth, we recommend waiting to use daily fluoride products at home until your child is old enough to spit reliably. To provide a safe cavity-fighting boost, we apply a professional, highly concentrated topical fluoride varnish at the end of our routine cleaning visits.
faqPage.group2q0: When should my child see an orthodontist?
faqPage.group2a0: The American Association of Orthodontists recommends that children undergo their first orthodontic screening by age seven. At this developmental stage, we can monitor skeletal jaw growth, track permanent tooth eruption, and spot potential alignment issues early. Early interceptive orthodontics — which may include specialized oral appliances or habit correctors — can guide jaw development and often dramatically shorten or simplify future orthodontic treatments.
faqPage.group2q1: Does my child still need dental checkups every six months if they have braces?
faqPage.group2a1: Yes, absolutely! Orthodontic brackets and wires easily trap food particles and sticky plaque, significantly increasing your child's risk of tooth decay and gum irritation. Regular professional checkups and cleanings remain vital to ensure their teeth stay healthy beneath their appliances. For children who struggle with daily brushing and flossing during orthodontic treatment, we frequently recommend cleanings on a more frequent schedule — every 3 to 4 months.
faqPage.group3q0: My child has a dental emergency — what should I do?
faqPage.group3a0: Call our office immediately at (847) 223-1400. We reserve dedicated emergency openings every day to treat kids in pain as soon as possible. Our experienced pediatric dental team will coordinate a prompt appointment — often same-day — and walk you through basic first-aid instructions over the phone to stabilize your child until you arrive.
faqPage.group3q1: What should I do if my child knocks out a permanent tooth?
faqPage.group3a1: Act immediately — time is the single most critical factor in saving the tooth. Touch the tooth ONLY by the crown (the top biting surface) and never touch the root. If the tooth is clean, attempt to gently reinsert it directly back into its socket and have your child bite down gently on gauze to keep it in place. If you cannot reinsert it, place the tooth in a cup of cold milk or a tooth preservation kit to keep it moist. Do not store the tooth in plain water or acidic liquids. Contact us immediately at (847) 223-1400 and arrive within 30 to 60 minutes for the highest clinical success rate.
faqPage.stillHaveBody: Our friendly team is happy to chat. Give us a call or send us a message.

contactPage.title: Contact Our Office
contactPage.subtitle: Questions, comments, or ready to book - we would love to hear from you.
contactPage.formHeading: We Would Love to Hear From You
contactPage.formBody: Fill out the form below and our team will be in touch within one business day. For urgent matters, please call us directly.
contactPage.placeholderMessage: How can we help you today?
contactPage.errorPrefix: Something went wrong - please call us at
contactPage.successBodySuffix: Our team will be in touch within one business day. If you need to reach us sooner, please call

preventivePage.title: Preventive Dentistry
preventivePage.subtitle: The best dental treatment is the kind that never needs to happen.
preventivePage.service0Desc: Professional cleanings remove tartar and plaque that brushing at home cannot reach. Every cleaning includes a thorough exam of teeth, gums, bite, jaw, and soft tissues - catching small issues before they become big ones.
preventivePage.service1Desc: Fluoride is nature's cavity fighter. A quick professional fluoride treatment after each cleaning strengthens enamel, reverses early-stage decay, and dramatically reduces the likelihood of cavities - especially during high-risk growth years.
preventivePage.service2Desc: The chewing surfaces of back molars are full of grooves where bacteria hide and thrive. Sealants are a thin, protective coating that bonds into those grooves and acts as a shield - proven to reduce cavity risk by up to 80% in the treated teeth.
preventivePage.service3Desc: Our digital X-ray system uses up to 90% less radiation than traditional film X-rays. Images are available instantly on-screen, so we can show you exactly what we see - and explain everything clearly - right during your visit.
preventivePage.service4Desc: The American Association of Orthodontists recommends a first orthodontic screening at age 7. Catching alignment issues early means simpler, shorter, and less expensive treatment - and avoids problems that compound as the jaw grows.
preventivePage.agesHeading: What to Expect at Every Stage
preventivePage.age0Detail: The first dental visit should happen when the first tooth appears or by your child's first birthday - whichever comes first. We check for early decay, advise on feeding habits, and set the stage for a lifetime of healthy routines.
preventivePage.age1Detail: Baby teeth matter more than many parents realize - they hold space for permanent teeth and affect speech development. We focus on cavity prevention and making every visit a positive, low-stress experience.
preventivePage.age2Detail: The transition from baby teeth to permanent teeth is one of the most important periods in dental development. We monitor eruption patterns, apply sealants to new molars, and perform early orthodontic screening.
preventivePage.age3Detail: Teens face unique challenges: orthodontic changes, increased sugar and sports drink exposure, and reduced parental supervision of home care habits. We provide honest, age-appropriate guidance without judgment.
preventivePage.faq0a: Yes! Fluoride strengthens enamel and prevents cavities - it is one of the most well-studied and safe tools in pediatric dentistry. We recommend a tiny smear of fluoride toothpaste for kids under 3, and a pea-sized amount for older children. Professional fluoride treatments at each cleaning visit provide an extra layer of protection exactly where your child needs it most.
preventivePage.faq1a: Dental sealants are a thin protective coating painted directly onto the chewing surfaces of back teeth - the molars where cavities most often start. The sealant flows into the grooves and hardens, acting as a shield against bacteria and food particles. The procedure is quick, easy, and completely pain-free, and sealants have been shown to reduce cavity risk in treated teeth by up to 80%.
preventivePage.faq2q: How often should my child visit the dentist?
preventivePage.faq2a: The American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry recommends a routine checkup and cleaning every 6 months to maintain optimal oral health. Some children with higher cavity risk may benefit from more frequent visits - we will give you a personalized recommendation at your child's first exam.
preventivePage.ctaText: Ready to set your child up for a lifetime of healthy smiles?

restorativePage.title: Restorative Dentistry
restorativePage.subtitle: Gentle, precise treatment that repairs damage, relieves pain, and protects your child's smile for years to come.
restorativePage.treatmentsHeading: The Right Treatment for Every Situation
restorativePage.treatment0Desc: When a cavity is caught early, a tooth-colored composite filling is all that's needed. We use composite resin that matches your child's natural tooth shade, and we also offer amalgam restorations upon parent's request. The procedure is quick, gentle, and often surprises both kids and parents with how manageable it is.
restorativePage.treatment1Desc: When decay is more advanced or a tooth is significantly weakened, a crown provides full coverage protection. Dental crowns are designed to last until the baby tooth naturally falls out. The most common crowns in pediatric dentistry are stainless steel crowns.
restorativePage.treatment2Desc: When decay reaches the nerve of a baby tooth, pulp therapy removes the infected tissue to save the tooth. Preserving baby teeth is important: they hold space for permanent teeth and are essential for proper chewing and speech development. The procedure is gentler than it sounds.
restorativePage.treatment3Desc: Sometimes a tooth is too damaged to save, or a baby tooth needs to come out to make room for an erupting permanent tooth. When an extraction is necessary, we perform it with care, precision, and appropriate anesthesia - ensuring your child feels nothing beyond a light pressure sensation.
restorativePage.treatment4Desc: If your child loses one of their back baby teeth earlier than expected, our pediatric dentists may recommend a space maintainer. As the name suggests, this appliance helps preserve the space left by the missing tooth so the permanent tooth has room to erupt properly. When a baby tooth is lost too soon, neighboring teeth often begin to shift or drift into the empty space. This movement can lead to crowding, misalignment, and may prevent the permanent tooth from coming in where it should. A space maintainer helps keep the surrounding teeth in their proper positions, reducing the risk of future orthodontic problems and potentially minimizing the need for more extensive treatment later. Fortunately, space maintainers are generally not necessary when front baby teeth are lost prematurely, as these teeth do not typically affect the eruption of permanent teeth in the same way.
restorativePage.ctaHeading: Cavities don't get smaller on their own.
restorativePage.ctaBody: The sooner we see your child, the simpler the treatment. Book an appointment today and let us take a look.

sedationPage.title: Sedation Dentistry
sedationPage.subtitle: Because every child deserves dental care that is calm, safe, and completely manageable.
sedationPage.quickAnswerBody: It's perfectly normal for children to feel nervous about visiting the dentist — and many adults feel the same way! At Kids Dentist, we do everything we can to help your child feel relaxed, comfortable, and at ease during their visit. However, some children experience anxiety that makes it difficult to safely complete treatment. In those situations, our pediatric dentists may recommend options such as nitrous oxide ("laughing gas") or general anesthesia to help your child have a comfortable, stress-free experience. We'll discuss all of the options with you and recommend the approach that best fits your child's needs.
sedationPage.whoKicker: Is This Right for Your Child?
sedationPage.whoHeading: Sedation May Be Appropriate If Your Child…
sedationPage.who0: Children with significant dental anxiety or phobia
sedationPage.who1: Kids with autism, sensory processing differences, or ADHD
sedationPage.who2: Children with complex medical histories or special needs
sedationPage.who3: Patients who need extensive dental work in one visit
sedationPage.who4: Very young children who are not yet developmentally ready to cooperate
sedationPage.who5: Children who have had a traumatic dental experience in the past
sedationPage.option0Desc: Nitrous oxide is a mild sedative inhaled through a small nose mask during treatment. It produces a pleasant, relaxed feeling - children often describe it as feeling "floaty" or "funny." It does not put your child to sleep; they remain awake and can respond to directions.
sedationPage.option0How: Effects begin within minutes and wear off completely within 5 minutes of removing the mask. Children can eat normally beforehand and return to school or activities immediately after.
sedationPage.option0Ideal: Mild to moderate anxiety, short procedures, children who are cooperative but nervous.
sedationPage.option1Desc: We work with a specialized anesthesiologist who comes to our office to provide excellent care to our patients. We offer nitrous oxide and general anesthesia.
sedationPage.option1How: Performed in our office with full monitoring equipment. Children are completely asleep and have no awareness or memory of the procedure. A thorough pre-operative consultation is required to review medical history and determine candidacy.
sedationPage.option1Ideal: Children with severe anxiety, extensive treatment needs, special needs, complex medical backgrounds, or who have not responded to lighter sedation.
sedationPage.faqHeading: Questions We Hear from Every Parent
sedationPage.faq0q: Do you offer sedation for nervous kids?
sedationPage.faq0a: Yes! We have safe, gentle options — including laughing gas (nitrous oxide) — to help kids feel completely relaxed and comfortable during their visit. For children who need a deeper level of support, we also offer general anesthesia. We will discuss all options with you and recommend the approach that best fits your child's needs.
sedationPage.faq1q: Is sedation safe for my child?
sedationPage.faq1a: Absolutely. Our board-certified pediatric dentists are highly trained in safely administering and monitoring child-appropriate sedation. We follow strict American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry guidelines, conduct thorough pre-operative health screenings, use age- and weight-appropriate dosing, and monitor vital signs throughout every procedure. Safety is always our first priority.
sedationPage.drHeading: Led by Dr. Dave Rutcosky, DDS, MS
sedationPage.drBody: Dr. Rutcosky has over 20 years of experience treating children with complex needs and leads our sedation program. Families throughout Lake County trust him to provide care that is truly impossible to access elsewhere.
sedationPage.ctaText: Not sure where to start? Let's talk through your child's situation together.
sedationPage.ctaAppointment: Talk to Us About Sedation Options

specialNeedsPage.title: Special Needs Dentistry
specialNeedsPage.subtitle: Every child deserves excellent dental care - regardless of their needs, challenges, or history.
specialNeedsPage.quickAnswerBody: Kids Dentist in Grayslake, IL provides specialized dental care for children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD), Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, ADHD, sensory processing differences, and complex medical histories. Our adapted environment, Tell-Show-Do communication, and in-office sedation options make quality dental care accessible for every child across Lake County.
specialNeedsPage.serveHeading: Experience Across a Wide Range of Needs
specialNeedsPage.approach0Desc: Every step of the appointment is narrated before it happens. We show your child each instrument, explain what it does in simple, friendly language, and only proceed when your child is ready. This approach removes the element of surprise and builds trust at every visit.
specialNeedsPage.approach1Desc: When a child needs a higher level of support, we offer nitrous oxide and general anesthesia. No child is ever denied the care they need because of anxiety or behavioral differences.
specialNeedsPage.approach2Desc: We know that you are the expert on your child. Before every appointment, we ask about your child's specific triggers, preferences, and communication style. That information shapes everything we do from the moment you walk in the door.
specialNeedsPage.ctaKicker: We Are Here for Your Family
specialNeedsPage.ctaHeading: Ready to find out how we can help?
specialNeedsPage.ctaBody: Call us to talk through your child's specific needs before booking. We want to make sure your first visit is set up for success.

emergencyPage.title: Emergency Dental Care
emergencyPage.subtitle: When your child is in pain or has had a dental injury, call us right away — we're here to help.
emergencyPage.alertPrefix: If your child is experiencing a life-threatening medical emergency, please
emergencyPage.alertSuffix: This page covers dental emergencies — we hold same-day dental slots, but we are not a substitute for emergency medical services.
emergencyPage.urgentBody: We do our best to see emergency patients the same day.
emergencyPage.isEmergencyBody: If your child is in pain or something looks wrong, that is reason enough to call. We would rather hear from you and help over the phone than have you wait it out.
emergencyPage.step0Desc: If your child has a dental emergency, call our office right away. We do our best to see emergency patients the same day so they don't have to stay in pain. Our team will also give you instructions on how to care for your child until you arrive.
emergencyPage.step1Desc: When you arrive, we'll bring your child in as quickly as possible. One of our pediatric dentists will examine their teeth and gums, identify the problem, and explain the recommended treatment.
emergencyPage.step2Desc: Our goal is to relieve your child's discomfort and restore their oral health. If additional treatment is needed, we'll schedule any follow-up visits before you leave and make sure you know exactly what to expect.
emergencyPage.tipBody: Find the tooth and hold it by the crown — the top part, not the root. If possible, carefully reinsert it into the socket and have your child hold it gently in place. If you cannot reinsert it, place the tooth in a cup of cold milk and call us immediately. Time is critical — the sooner we see your child, the better the chance of saving the tooth.
emergencyPage.ctaHeading: Dental emergency? Call us right away.
emergencyPage.ctaBody: During office hours, we do our best to see emergency patients the same day — new and existing patients welcome.

about.title: Trusted by Lake County Families
about.philosophyBody: Since 1994, Kids Dentist has been the pediatric dental home for Grayslake and Lake County. We've watched whole families grow up here — children who are now bringing their own kids in for that very first appointment. Our four board-certified pediatric specialists have spent their entire careers focused on one mission: give every child the healthiest possible start and a dental experience they'll actually look forward to.
about.doctorsBody: Every doctor at Kids Dentist completed a full pediatric dental residency after dental school — specialized post-graduate training focused exclusively on children at every stage of development, including kids with anxiety, sensory sensitivities, and special needs.
about.soniaBio: Dr. Gutierrez graduated at the top of her class from the Health Sciences University (CES) in Colombia, then completed her pediatric dentistry residency and earned her Master of Science in Oral Sciences at the University of Illinois at Chicago. She is a Board-Certified Pediatric Dentist and Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry with over 18 years of experience caring for children in Lake County.
about.soniaAlt: Dr. Sonia Gutierrez, board-certified pediatric dentist at Kids Dentist in Grayslake, Illinois
about.daveBio: Dr. Rutcosky is a lifelong Chicagoland resident who earned his dental degree from Northwestern University before completing his specialty and Master's degree in Oral Sciences at the University of Illinois at Chicago. He is a Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry.
about.daveAlt: Dr. Dave Rutcosky, board-certified pediatric dentist at Kids Dentist in Grayslake, Illinois
about.saharBio: Dr. Alrayyes is a full-time Clinical Associate Professor and Clinic Director at the University of Illinois Department of Pediatric Dentistry, where she earned her MS in Oral Sciences. She is a Diplomate and Fellow of the American Board and Academy of Pediatric Dentistry.
about.saharAlt: Dr. Sahar Alrayyes, board-certified pediatric dentist at Kids Dentist in Grayslake, Illinois
about.anneAshleyBio: Dr. Compton earned her dental degree from Virginia Commonwealth University before completing her specialty and Master's degree in Oral Sciences at the University of Illinois at Chicago. She is a Board-Certified Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry.
about.anneAshleyAlt: Dr. Anne-Ashley Compton, board-certified pediatric dentist at Kids Dentist in Grayslake, Illinois
about.tourKicker: Designed for Kids, Built for Parents
about.tourHeading: An Office That Feels Nothing Like a Dentist's Office
about.tourBody: From the moment you walk through the door, you'll know something is different here. We designed every space — from the welcoming waiting area to the soothing treatment bays — to feel warm, unhurried, and completely non-clinical.
about.tourFeature0Heading: A Waiting Room Worth Arriving Early For
about.tourFeature0Body: Bright colors, kid-scaled furniture, and play areas that make the wait disappear. Parents get comfortable seating and a calm, unhurried atmosphere — no rushed energy, no clinical anxiety in sight.
about.tourFeature0Alt: Welcoming waiting room at Kids Dentist Grayslake with colorful child-friendly furniture and play areas
about.tourFeature1Body: Overhead TVs in every room let kids choose what they watch. Temperature, lighting, and pace are all calibrated around the child. Parents are always welcome to stay — no separation, no surprises.
about.tourFeature1Alt: Soothing treatment bay at Kids Dentist Grayslake with overhead TV and child-centered equipment

meetDentists.title: Meet Our Dentists
meetDentists.subtitle: Four pediatric specialists who genuinely love what they do. Click any card to learn more.
meetDentists.ctaBody: Our team of four specialists is here to make every visit a positive experience. New patients always welcome.

drProfiles.sonia_heroHeading: Warmth, Expertise, and a Smile That Puts Kids at Ease
drProfiles.sonia_bio0: Dr. Sonia Gutierrez has been caring for young smiles in Lake County for over 30 years. When you want a pediatric dentist who will be sure to take her time with your son or daughter, Dr. Sonia is absolutely the right choice. She loves caring for younger patients because they're always genuine, loving, honest, and fun! By going slow, she's able to show them that visiting the dentist's office doesn't have to be scary or hard.
drProfiles.sonia_bio1: Dr. Sonia graduated at the top of her class in dental school from the Health Sciences University (CES) in her native Colombia. From there, she attended a pediatric dentistry residency at the University of Illinois, Chicago College of Dentistry, followed by the completion of her Master of Science from the same university. Today, she is a Board-Certified Pediatric Dentist and a Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry. Dr. Sonia feels that it is absolutely essential to stay up to date with new technologies and techniques in pediatric dentistry, which is why she participates in countless trainings throughout each year.
drProfiles.sonia_bio2: Dr. Sonia finds it extremely exciting to watch a patient grow up and see their personality develop and emerge! She tends to form very strong attachments with each child — in fact, they feel like a member of her family! That's what makes it so hard when they eventually become young adults and have to say goodbye and transition to an adult dentist. When she isn't working or studying, Dr. Sonia's top priority is spending quality time with her family, which includes her husband, two daughters, and one son. She is also extremely passionate about women's education, empowering women, traveling, learning new things, and cooking, and gardening.
drProfiles.sonia_quote: The first dental visit sets the tone for a lifetime. It is my goal to make the dental experience better for every child who walks through the door.
drProfiles.sonia_cred0: Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS), Health Sciences University (CES), Colombia — Graduated Top of Class

drProfiles.dave_heroHeading: Ensuring No Child Is Ever Left Behind
drProfiles.dave_bio0: Dr. Dave has been an integral part of the Kids Dentist team for many years! A lot of parents are relieved to find a dentist who's extremely qualified and comfortable treating children with special needs, from those with dental anxiety to autism, Down syndrome, cerebral palsy, and more. These children deserve to receive the same excellent dental care as any other child, and Dr. Dave ensures they receive just that.
drProfiles.dave_bio1: Dr. Dave graduated from Northwestern University with his Doctorate of Dental Surgery and completed his specialty training and residency in Pediatric Dentistry at the University of Illinois at Chicago, where he also earned a Master of Science in Oral Sciences. He is a Board-Certified Pediatric Dentist and was extremely motivated to achieve board certification because of his desire to advance the field and provide patients with exceptional dental care.
drProfiles.dave_bio2: An important part of Dr. Dave's approach to dentistry is communication. He takes the time to clearly explain what he'll be doing throughout the appointment, particularly to children with special needs and their parents — because when everyone feels at ease and knows what to expect, the appointment itself goes much more smoothly. When not treating patients, Dr. Dave enjoys spending time with friends and family. He also has a passion for sports, travel, and music.
drProfiles.dave_quote: Every child deserves excellent dental care — regardless of their needs, challenges, or fears. I am privileged to be the doctor who makes that possible for families who often do not know where else to turn.

drProfiles.sahar_heroHeading: Prevention First — A Lifetime of Healthy Smiles Starts Early
drProfiles.sahar_bio0: Dr. Sahar Alrayyes has been part of the Kids Dentist family for over 15 years and has been a pillar of its excellence, teaching, and compassionate care. In addition to her clinical work at Kids Dentist, she is a full-time Clinical Associate Professor and the Clinic Director at the University of Illinois Department of Pediatric Dentistry.
drProfiles.sahar_bio1: She earned her Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) from Northwestern University Dental School in 1998 and completed her specialty training in Pediatric Dentistry and her Master of Science in Oral Sciences at the University of Illinois at Chicago in 2002. Dr. Sahar is a Diplomate of the American Board of Pediatric Dentistry and a Fellow of the American Academy of Pediatric Dentistry.
drProfiles.sahar_bio2: Dr. Sahar is deeply passionate about volunteer care and travels to underprivileged counties at least twice each year to provide volunteer pediatric dental care. In her free time, she enjoys spending quality time with her husband and their two boys, biking around the city, and playing soccer.
drProfiles.sahar_quote: Healthy teeth start before the first tooth. I love helping parents understand what to do — and what to look for — from day one. That early guidance changes everything.

drProfiles.anneAshley_heroHeading: Precision, Patience, and the Art of Making Kids Comfortable
drProfiles.anneAshley_bio0: Dr. Ashley has wanted to care for children for as long as she can remember. Born and raised in Southern Virginia, she completed her undergraduate degree at North Carolina State University and went on to earn her Doctor of Dental Surgery degree at Virginia Commonwealth University in Richmond, VA.
drProfiles.anneAshley_bio1: She then moved to Chicago to complete a two-year specialty residency program in Pediatric Dentistry at the University of Illinois at Chicago, where she also earned a Master's degree in Oral Sciences.
drProfiles.anneAshley_bio2: Dr. Ashley really enjoys being able to create a positive, comfortable experience for each child in need. Helping them think of the dentist's office as a fun place is a great feeling! She also enjoys volunteering to provide oral care to kids in underserved areas and countries around the world. In her spare time, Dr. Ashley enjoys traveling and spending quality time with friends and family. She's also an avid sports fan!
drProfiles.anneAshley_quote: When a child leaves my chair smiling — not just because we are done, but because it really was not bad at all — that is the moment I live for. That is what this career is about.
